questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.explanation: The article focuses on learning, badges, and certification, not on selling templates.

options.text: To provide learning paths, badges, and certifications for Notion users
options.explanation: Correct – the opening paragraph states Notion Academy turns curiosity into capability by offering learning paths, badges, and certifications.

options.text: To host live webinars exclusively for enterprise customers
options.explanation: Webinars are mentioned, but they are available to all users, not only enterprises.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center entirely
options.explanation: The Help Center is still referenced for detailed questions; Notion Academy complements it.

questions.question: Which certification does Notion Academy offer?

options.explanation: Design certification is not mentioned in the article.
The article explicitly mentions becoming a Notion Certified Admin.
Developer certification is not listed.
Analyst certification is not part of the described offerings.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a featured lesson in the article?

options.explanation: Custom Agents appears as a featured lesson.
That lesson is listed.
Project management is included among the lessons.
No lesson with that title is mentioned in the article.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the "Essentials Path"?

options.explanation: The article lists 3 lessons for the Essentials Path.
Correct – the Essentials Path is described as having 3 lessons.
4 lessons correspond to the Workflows Path, not Essentials.
5 lessons belong to the Advanced Path.

questions.question: Which path is targeted at an intermediate skill level and focuses on creating connected systems?

options.explanation: Essentials is beginner‑level.
The Workflows Path is labeled intermediate and mentions creating connected systems.
Advanced Path is for advanced users.
There is no separate "Beginner Path" listed; Essentials fulfills that role.

questions.question: What is the typical duration of the "Templates & marketplace" lesson?

options.explanation: The article lists 40 minutes for this lesson.
30 minutes is the length of the Custom Agents lesson.
Correct – the Templates & marketplace lesson is described as 40 min.
No lesson in the article is listed as 50 minutes.

questions.question: Which statement about the Notion Certified Admin certification is true?

options.text: It is only available for Enterprise plan users.
options.explanation: The article says the certification is for any plan type.

options.text: It recognizes admins for any plan type at any company.
options.explanation: Correct – the text mentions certification is available for any plan type at any company.

options.text: It requires completing the Advanced Path first.
options.explanation: The certification is not tied to a specific path in the article.

options.text: It is free for all Notion users.
options.explanation: The article does not state that the certification is free.

questions.question: Which beginner‑level lesson teaches how to share, collaborate, and brainstorm with team members?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is a beginner lesson but focuses on automation, not collaboration.
Correct – the lesson title directly matches the described content.
Workspace design is an advanced lesson.
That lesson is intermediate and focuses on knowledge systems.

questions.question: If a user wants to learn how to build an intake system using forms, charts, and automations, which lesson should they take and at what skill level?

options.explanation: Custom Agents covers automation but not specifically intake systems.

options.text: "An issue tracker for any team" – Intermediate
options.explanation: Correct – the description states this lesson teaches building an intake system with forms, charts, and automations at the intermediate level.
Workspace design focuses on design best practices, not intake systems.
That lesson is about scaling knowledge, not intake forms.

questions.question: Which path would best suit a user who wants to automate work and learn workspace architecture best practices?

options.explanation: Essentials covers basics, not advanced automation.
Workflows focuses on connected systems but not explicitly on advanced automation and architecture.
Correct – the Advanced Path description mentions automating work and learning workspace architecture best practices.
There is no separate "Custom Agents Path" listed.
